findings.title: Signal handler uses asyncio.get_event_loop() which returns None in Python 3.10+ after asyncio.run()
findings.suggested_fix: Store the event loop reference before calling asyncio.run() or use a threading.Event to signal shutdown. The signal handler runs in the main thread but asyncio.run() creates a new loop. Store loop in a global or use loop.call_soon_threadsafe() with a stored loop reference. Pattern: `loop = asyncio.new_event_loop(); asyncio.set_event_loop(loop); loop.run_until_complete(run_headless(...))` and reference that loop in the handler.

findings.title: macOS disable() does not call launchctl remove, leaving service registered until reboot
findings.suggested_fix: After launchctl unload, also call `launchctl remove <label>` to fully remove the service from launchd's memory. The unload command only stops the current run; remove ensures it won't be remembered. Add: `subprocess.run(['launchctl', 'remove', label], capture_output=True, timeout=5)` after successful unload.

findings.title: Docker detection false negative: podman and other container runtimes not detected
findings.suggested_fix: Add detection for additional container runtimes in cgroup check: 'podman', 'containerd', 'crio', 'lxc'. Also check for environment variables like 'container=podman' or 'KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST' which indicate container environments.

findings.title: Race condition in _toggle_auto_start: state desynchronized if disable/enable fails silently
findings.suggested_fix: Check return value of enable()/disable() and only update _auto_start if operation succeeded. Change to: `if self._autostart_manager.is_enabled(): result = self._autostart_manager.disable(); self._auto_start = not result if not result else self._auto_start` or simpler: re-query `self._auto_start = self._autostart_manager.is_enabled()` after the operation.

findings.title: Windows registry key not closed on exception in enable/disable/is_enabled
findings.suggested_fix: Use context manager pattern for registry key: `with winreg.OpenKey(...) as key:` instead of manual OpenKey/CloseKey. This ensures the key is closed even if an exception occurs between operations.

findings.title: clipboard check has false negative: reading empty clipboard raises no exception but returns empty string
findings.suggested_fix: pyperclip.paste() returns empty string for empty clipboard, not an exception. The current implementation is correct for detection purposes - an empty clipboard is still a valid clipboard. However, consider adding explicit check for pyperclip being importable as the primary test, as the paste() test may succeed on headless systems with xclip installed but no X server.

findings.title: SingleInstanceLock lock file race condition: multiple instances can pass the check simultaneously
findings.suggested_fix: Use file locking (fcntl.flock on Unix, msvcrt.locking or win32file.LockFileEx on Windows) for atomic test-and-set. The current check-then-create pattern has a TOCTOU race window. Alternatively, use O_EXCL flag when creating the file to ensure atomic creation.

findings.title: docker-entrypoint.sh wait command returns immediately if PID process already exited
findings.suggested_fix: Consider adding a small sleep or check if process exited cleanly before trap fires. The current implementation is correct for graceful shutdown, but if Python crashes immediately, the wait returns 0 and container exits before any useful error logging.

findings.title: has_clipboard() side effect: reads clipboard content which may be undesirable
findings.suggested_fix: Consider using pyperclip.is_available() if available, or catching and ignoring clipboard content. Reading clipboard during startup may log sensitive data if debug logging is enabled. Store result in a local variable and don't log it.

findings.title: Platform level 2 classification (no clipboard) may not match Level 3 (headless) behavior expectations
findings.suggested_fix: Review if Level 2 (has display, no clipboard) should actually fall back to headless. A system with display but broken clipboard (e.g., missing xsel) might be better served by Level 1 with degraded features rather than forcing headless. Consider if clipboard is truly required for tray functionality.

findings.title: Linux autostart Exec path may have quoting issues with spaces in path
findings.suggested_fix: In _get_executable_path(), for development mode, the path is wrapped in quotes but desktop entry Exec field requires special escaping. Use shlex.quote() for proper shell escaping: `exec_path = f'{shlex.quote(sys.executable)} {shlex.quote(str(Path(__file__).parent.parent / "main.py"))}'` and ensure the desktop entry handles this correctly.

findings.title: Missing SIGTERM handling in tray mode - only SIGINT handled via KeyboardInterrupt
findings.suggested_fix: In tray mode (run_with_tray), KeyboardInterrupt from Ctrl+C is caught but SIGTERM signal from external sources won't trigger cleanup. The signal_handler sets up handlers but they don't properly stop the tray. Need to call tray.stop() in signal handler or use threading.Event to signal the async loop to stop.

findings.title: lock variable may be referenced before assignment in finally block if exception occurs before assignment
findings.suggested_fix: Initialize lock = None before the try block starts (line 103). Currently, if an exception occurs between line 103 (in_docker check) and line 109 (lock assignment), the finally block will raise UnboundLocalError when checking 'if lock is not None'.

findings.title: macOS enable() does not load the LaunchAgent, requiring logout/login or manual load
findings.suggested_fix: After creating the plist file, call `launchctl load <plist_file>` to activate the agent immediately without requiring logout. Add: `subprocess.run(['launchctl', 'load', str(plist_file)], capture_output=True, timeout=5)` after writing the plist file.

findings.title: No validation that server.runner is set before using it in signal handler
findings.suggested_fix: The check `if server.runner is not None:` is correct, but consider also checking if the event loop is still running before calling run_coroutine_threadsafe. If the loop has already stopped, this call will fail. Add try/except around the call.

residual_risks.description: Docker detection is inherently heuristic and may have false positives/negatives with custom container configurations
residual_risks.impact: Users in custom container environments may get incorrect platform detection
residual_risks.mitigation: Allow manual override via environment variable (e.g., LLM_ROUTE_FORCE_HEADLESS=1)

residual_risks.description: Signal handling with asyncio has inherent complexity due to Python's signal handling model
residual_risks.impact: Race conditions in graceful shutdown may cause resource leaks or incomplete cleanup
residual_risks.mitigation: Consider using asyncio.Signal or a dedicated shutdown coordination mechanism

residual_risks.description: Cross-platform autostart depends on platform-specific behaviors that may change with OS updates
residual_risks.impact: Autostart may break after OS upgrades (e.g., macOS security changes, Linux desktop environment changes)
residual_risks.mitigation: Add version-specific handling and extensive testing matrix

testing_gaps.description: No integration tests for signal handling in headless vs tray mode
testing_gaps.recommendation: Add tests that send SIGTERM/SIGINT to running process and verify cleanup

testing_gaps.description: Tests only mock Path.exists(), no tests for actual cgroup content parsing
testing_gaps.recommendation: Add tests with realistic /proc/1/cgroup content for various container runtimes

testing_gaps.description: No tests for the _auto_start state variable remaining synchronized with actual state
testing_gaps.recommendation: Add tests that verify _auto_start matches is_enabled() after each operation

testing_gaps.description: No tests for asyncio.run() interaction with signal handlers
testing_gaps.recommendation: Add async test fixtures that verify signal handlers can properly stop the event loop
